incidents.title: SQL injection pattern in request path/query (2 event(s))
incidents.description: Rule SQLI-001 matched on field "path". Sources: 10.0.0.88.

incidents.description: Rule RECON-002 matched on field "path". Sources: 172.16.0.20.

incidents.title: SSH brute force from 10.0.0.50 SUCCEEDED (4 failures then login as 'admin')
incidents.description: 4 failed SSH authentications followed by an accepted login as 'admin' from the same IP. Treat the account as compromised.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:03 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52341 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:04 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52342 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:05 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52343 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:06 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52344 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:07 server sshd[1234]: Accepted password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52345 ssh2

incidents.title: Login brute force from 10.0.0.50 SUCCEEDED (4 failures then a successful login)
incidents.description: 4 failed login POSTs followed by HTTP 200 on the same endpoint ['/login'] — probable account takeover. Treat the account as compromised.

incidents.title: SSH username enumeration from 203.0.113.5 (3 attempts on invalid accounts)
incidents.description: Login attempts against non-existent users (root, test, ubuntu) — probing valid usernames, typical of a pre-brute-force sweep.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:09 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.5 port 44123 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:10 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for invalid user root from 203.0.113.5 port 44124 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:11 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for invalid user ubuntu from 203.0.113.5 port 44125 ssh2

incidents.title: Server throttled 10.0.0.99 with HTTP 429 (5 rapid requests to ['/api/users'])
incidents.description: The application returned 429 Too Many Requests — automated/scripted activity (e.g. mass account creation or API hammering) confirmed by the server's own rate limiter.

incidents.title: Vulnerability scan from 203.0.113.5 (4 admin/sensitive endpoints probed)
incidents.description: Probed sensitive paths: /admin, /admin/, /admin/../../../etc/passwd, /admin/config. Consistent with automated vulnerability/CMS scanning.

incidents.title: Vulnerability scan from 172.16.0.20 (6 admin/sensitive endpoints probed)
incidents.description: Probed sensitive paths: /.env, /admin, /administrator, /config.php, /phpmyadmin, /wp-admin. Consistent with automated vulnerability/CMS scanning.

incidents.title: Sensitive sudo command by 'johndoe': /bin/cat /etc/shadow
incidents.description: A successful sudo executed a command that reads credentials or alters privilege configuration (e.g. shadow/sudoers/SSH keys). Verify this was authorized.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:15 server sudo: johndoe : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/johndoe ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/cat /etc/shadow

incidents.title: 1 malformed / unparseable line(s)
incidents.description: Lines that matched no known format in: webserver.log. Could indicate log tampering, corruption, or an unsupported format.

incidents.title: Multi-stage attack from 203.0.113.5: Reconnaissance → Path Traversal
incidents.description: IP 203.0.113.5 appears in 3 separate detections across 2 log files (auth2.log, webserver.log). Coordinated activity spanning multiple attack stages.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:09 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.5 port 44123 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:10 server sshd[1235]: Failed password for invalid user root from 203.0.113.5 port 44124 ssh2

incidents.title: Coordinated multi-service attack from 10.0.0.50 (Brute Force Attacks against 2 services)
incidents.description: IP 10.0.0.50 appears in 2 separate detections across 2 log files (auth2.log, webserver.log). Coordinated activity spanning multiple attack stages.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:03 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52341 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:04 server sshd[1234]: Failed password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52342 ssh2

analyst_observations.title: 'Sensitive sudo command by 'johndoe': /bin/cat /etc/shadow' occurred 8s after a confirmed compromise from 10.0.0.50
analyst_observations.description: Timeline proximity between a compromise and a subsequent sensitive action by a different identity. This matches the classic post-exploitation sequence (initial access → credential access), but the logs do not prove the two are connected. Recommend investigating whether the identities are linked.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:07 server sshd[1234]: Accepted password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52345 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:15 server sudo: johndoe : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/johndoe ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/bin/cat /etc/shadow

analyst_observations.title: Session inconsistency: 10.0.0.50:52345 closed '[preauth]' but the same port previously authenticated
analyst_observations.description: A '[preauth]' close means the connection ended before authentication, yet this exact source IP:port pair produced an accepted login earlier. The line parses cleanly, so it is not flagged as malformed — but it is semantically contradictory. Possible log artifact, clock/ordering issue, or tampering. Recommend comparing against a second log source.
evidence: Jul  3 10:00:07 server sshd[1234]: Accepted password for admin from 10.0.0.50 port 52345 ssh2
Jul  3 10:00:25 server sshd[1245]: Connection closed by 10.0.0.50 port 52345 [preauth]
